summary: Iteration 4 retest of Forge IDE agent-mode write flow. Verified the inline 'Agent reminder' postfix is present in ChatPanel.jsx (line 129) and is appended to the user message in agent mode. However, the claimed temperature lowering to 0.2 was NOT actually applied — api.js line 51 still sends temperature: 0.4. Most importantly, the end-to-end agent write flow STILL fails: meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct continues to reply conversationally ('I'm ready to assist. What is your request?') instead of emitting a <forge-file> block, so hello.txt is not created.
passed: data-testid='chat-input' accepts text; data-testid='chat-send-btn' sends the message; streamed reply renders in the chat panel
Inline 'Agent reminder' postfix is present in /app/frontend/src/ide/components/ChatPanel.jsx (line 129)

failed.issue: Agent STILL does not emit any <forge-file> block for the canonical few-shot prompt 'Create a file named hello.txt with the text Hi there'. Model replied conversationally: "I'm ready to assist. What is your request?" The inline agent reminder postfix had no observable effect on the model's behavior.
failed.evidence: Polled GET /api/fs/read?path=hello.txt every 2s for 60s after sending the message — only HTTP 404 observed. Visible chat reply: 'I'm ready to assist. What is your request?'. Screenshot: .screenshots/agent_reply_iter4.png.

failed.issue: Problem statement says temperature was lowered to 0.2, but /app/frontend/src/ide/lib/api.js line 51 still sends temperature: 0.4. The temperature change was not actually committed.
failed.evidence: grep on api.js: "body: JSON.stringify({ model, messages, system, temperature: 0.4, max_tokens: 2048 })"

action_items: Apply the missing temperature change: in /app/frontend/src/ide/lib/api.js line 51 change temperature: 0.4 → 0.2 (or lower, e.g. 0.0) and retest.

The inline reminder + few-shot system prompt has now been tried twice with no behavioral change on meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct via NVIDIA NIM. Recommend: (a) verify in backend logs / DevTools Network tab that the final outbound request actually contains both the AGENT_SYSTEM (in system role) and the reminder-suffixed user message, (b) try moving the reminder to the FRONT of the user message (prefix) rather than the end, (c) try a stronger instruction-following default model (e.g. meta/llama-3.1-405b-instruct or a Qwen2.5-Coder variant) and confirm whether the issue is model-side or prompt-routing-side.
Consider adding an automated assertion that ChatPanel.jsx's outbound payload contains '[Agent reminder' so future regressions are caught.

success_rate.frontend: 60% (5 of 6 UI/integration checks passed; agent write flow + temperature change both broken)
